expectations.text: Layout uses Flex, Grid, Container, or Box from hearth-react. No raw <div> used as structural containers.
expectations.evidence: Uses Container, Flex, Grid, Box (marginLeft='auto' wrapper) — all from hearth-react. No raw <div> anywhere in the file.

expectations.text: Card (or CardActionLink/CardContent sub-components) from hearth-react is used for each service.
expectations.evidence: Each service is rendered by ServiceCard which uses Card, CardContent, CardInteraction. TotalSpendSummary also uses Card+CardContent. Empty state uses Card+CardContent.

expectations.text:
Heading, BodyText, or DetailText from hearth-react used for all text. No raw

-

or

.
expectations.evidence: Heading for page title and service names, BodyText for secondary labels, DetailText size='2xl' for individual service costs, DetailText size={{ mobile: '3xl', desktop: '4xl' }} for total spend. No raw text elements.

expectations.text: Responsive object notation used on at least one prop (e.g. direction={{ mobile: 'column', desktop: 'row' }} or gridColumnSpan={{ mobile: 'full', desktop: '4' }}) to handle mobile vs desktop layout.
expectations.evidence: Multiple responsive props: columns={{ mobile: 1, tablet: 2, desktop: 3 }}, gap={{ mobile: '300', desktop: '400' }}, direction={{ mobile: 'column', tablet: 'row' }}, size={{ mobile: 'lg', desktop: 'xl' }}, paddingY={{ mobile: '400', desktop: '800' }}, size={{ mobile: '3xl', desktop: '4xl' }}.

expectations.text: Total monthly spend is calculated from the services and displayed prominently, using an appropriate Hearth typography component.
expectations.evidence: totalSpend = services.reduce((sum, svc) => sum + svc.monthlyCost, 0). Displayed in TotalSpendSummary via <DetailText size={{ mobile: '3xl', desktop: '4xl' }} as='p'>. Conditionally shown only when services.length > 0.

expectations.text: Each service's manage link uses Link, CardActionLink, or CardInteraction from hearth-react. No raw <a> tags.
expectations.evidence: <CardInteraction asChild><Link href={manageHref}>Manage {name}<ChevronRightSmallIcon /></Link></CardInteraction> for each service card. Empty state also uses CardInteraction+Link.

expectations.text: A ServicesDashboard.test.tsx file is present and contains at least one test.
expectations.evidence: ServicesDashboard.test.tsx present with 18 tests across 6 describe blocks: page heading, total spend summary, service cards, empty state, currency formatting, accessibility.

claims.claim: Component accepts services as props (data-driven) rather than hardcoding them
claims.evidence: ServicesDashboardProps has customerName?: string and services: Service[]. The component is fully data-driven and testable with any service data.

claims.claim: Empty state is handled with a dedicated UI rather than rendering nothing
claims.evidence: {services.length === 0 ? <Card>No active services...</Card> : <Grid>...services...</Grid>} — explicit empty state with message and explore link.

claims.claim: DetailText is used for price values instead of Heading
claims.evidence: DetailText size='2xl' for individual service costs and DetailText size={{ mobile: '3xl', desktop: '4xl' }} for total spend — semantically correct numeric display component.

suggestions.reason: No assertion checks whether the component accepts services as props vs hardcodes them. This is a significant quality difference — the with-skill output is reusable, the without-skill output requires code changes to display real data.
No assertion checks for an empty state. The with-skill output includes a proper empty state; the without-skill output does not. This is an important UX consideration that goes unchecked.

eval_feedback.overall: All assertions pass. The with-skill output is notably higher quality: data-driven props, empty state handling, DetailText for prices, comprehensive responsive breakpoints. The assertions miss these quality signals.
